mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future earnings, depressing equity valuations. With the FEDFUNDS at 4.09% during a recession, this restrictive monetary stance signals persistent headwinds, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 20-day horizon is not significantly negative (p < 0.05) in out-of-sample periods, or the top quintile of FEDFUNDS levels does not produce lower returns than the bottom quintile.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds a critical threshold (5.0%), it signals that labor market slack is severe enough to depress consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE > 5.0% compared to when UNRATE ≤ 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and retail sales over three months indicate a synchronized contraction in both supply and demand, which typically foreshadows further economic deterioration and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month decline in both INDPRO and RSAFS
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not negative (i.e., mean return ≥ 0) when both INDPRO and RSAFS have declined over the prior three months, across all such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is declining (monetary easing) and inflation is low (CPI below 2% year-over-year), real interest rates fall sharply, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations. This combination produces positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declining over 3 months and CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2%
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with low inflation
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's rank IC over a 3-year rolling window stays below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in payroll employment (PAYEMS) directly reduce aggregate income and consumer spending, which erodes corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month, irrespective of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has been trending down in the recession
failure_condition: Monthly changes in PAYEMS do not show a significant negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns (Pearson r > -0.1 fails).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,000 thousand units annualized reflect a severe housing market depression that continues to weigh on construction, employment, and household wealth, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter as the drag persists.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when HOUST < 1000 are not significantly lower than when HOUST ≥ 1000 (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in industrial production (INDPRO declining over 3 months) and labor market (UNRATE rising over 3 months) signals a deep and broad economic contraction that reliably predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month, as corporate earnings and investor sentiment collapse.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declining over 3 months and UNRATE rising over 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of instances when both conditions hold, or the average return is not significantly below zero (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are at a 12-month low and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is at a 12-month high, the economy is likely at or near a recession trough. Forward-looking markets anticipate recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS at 12-month low and UNRATE at 12-month high
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) in months when both conditions are met, or the strategy's Sharpe ratio is less than 0.5 over the full sample.
